page: Microsoft Azure FX8-2mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine belonging to the FXmdsv2 family. It is powered by an Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor running at 4.2 GHz, configured with 1 physical core and 2 vCPUs via Microsoft Hyper-V. The system features a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets, including AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX). It is equipped with 168.0 GB of RAM, yielding an exceptionally high memory-to-core ratio of 84.0 GB per physical core. Local storage is provided via a 472 GB disk, and the instance does not include a GPU.

Benchmark data reveals a stark contrast between single-core and multi-core performance. The processor achieves top-tier single-core results, with a Geekbench single-core score of 2344.0 and strong single-threaded performance in gzip compression and bzip3 decompression. Conversely, multi-core benchmarks are weak to poor, with a stress-ng multi-core score in the bottom 10% and a Geekbench multi-core score in the bottom 25%. Memory bandwidth also presents a bottleneck, with large-block read-only bandwidth registering in the bottom 10% at 21.09 GB/sec, though small-block cached reads perform slightly better.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the FX8-2mds_v2 offers high memory density relative to its compute footprint, making it a specialized choice for workloads that require massive memory capacity but minimal parallel processing. This hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive, low-concurrency applications, single-threaded database operations, in-memory caching, and text processing tasks that fit entirely within RAM but do not scale across multiple CPU cores.
description: Microsoft Azure FX8-2mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine from the FXmdsv2 family, featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C x86_64 processor with 2 vCPUs (1 physical core) running at 4.2 GHz. It is provisioned with 168.0 GB of RAM, a 472 GB local disk, and no GPU. Benchmarks show a significant divergence in performance: single-core metrics are strong, with a top-tier Geekbench single-core score of 2344.0 and strong single-threaded compression, whereas multi-core and memory bandwidth metrics are weak to poor. This server is designed for memory-intensive, single-threaded workloads, such as low-concurrency database operations and text processing, where massive memory capacity is required but parallel compute demands are minimal.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX8-2mds_v2 is a memory-optimized VM featuring an Intel Xeon 8573C processor with 2 vCPUs, 168 GB RAM, and 472 GB local storage, delivering strong single-core but weak multi-core performance.

tagline: A memory-intensive virtual machine pairing an exceptional memory-to-core ratio with strong single-threaded performance for low-concurrency, high-capacity workloads.

168.0 GB of RAM providing an extreme 84.0 GB per physical core ratio
472 GB of local storage included without GPU acceleration
Top-tier single-core performance contrasted with weak multi-core capabilities
Suited for memory-intensive, single-threaded database and text-processing workloads
